essence: David debugs Ecamm Live's Twitter/X streaming destination failing with a silent spinner and no error logs despite his new X Premium account, dictating a support email to Ecamm asking how to access diagnostic logs.

actions.text: Write and send debug email to Ecamm support explaining Twitter/X auth spinner issue and asking how to check logs

essence: David live-tests his streaming setup, confirming the stream reaches YouTube Live on the AppyDave channel but remaining unsure whether anything reached Twitter/X, interleaved with household cleaning-product chatter.

David buys Twitter/X Premium (~$192) while spinning up a new twitterx second brain to understand Premium features, Grok terminal access, and X Pro, and asks whether Whisper's free developer tier could survive eight hours of daily streaming transcription.

actions.text: Create and verify the twitterx second brain covering Premium features, Grok access, and X Pro
Determine Whisper free developer plan limits for ~8h/day intermittent streaming transcription

essence: David debugs Ecamm Live hijacking his single HyperX QuadCast microphone when streaming starts — killing his voice agents mid-demo — and questions why routing one app to the HyperX Cast aggregate device in Audio MIDI Setup should resolve a conflict on the same physical mic.

David directs his assistant to create a second brain around an online inference engine and research its available models, usage patterns, and developer-token-plan costs, plus show the Ecamm Live folder structure.

actions.text: Create a second brain for the online inference engine and research models, usage, and developer-plan costs

essence: David states his professional positioning in one line: his real work is educating people in agentic AI coding and applications.

David plans an introduction email to Wiphu — a contact met the previous day via Dr. Pat in Chiang Mai — briefly explaining his AppyDave/Claude Code education work and proposing a one-hour catch-up next week to explore alignment.

actions.text: Send introduction email to Wiphu proposing a one-hour meeting next week

essence: David flags that the POEM 'external consultants' plugin must be renamed to 'consultants' (command, folder, versions, no remnants), that npx poem start errors, and that the port registry has inconsistent port/name/folder entries plus a mystery 'installation 17689'.

actions.text: Rename external-consultants plugin to consultants across command name, folder, and versions, leaving no remnants — research plugin docs first
Clean up port registry so every registered port has a name and folder, and identify what 'installation 17689' is

essence: David walks a teammate through his Whisper-based transcribe tool and a fresh POEM OS install (Astro 6 upgrade, port registry suggesting 9520 since 9500 is SupportSignal's), then coaches the teammate on Windows/WSL to git pull and resume Claude conversations (-c/-r) instead of restarting, leaving them to self-fix their environment.

actions.text: Fix POEM port registry to list all registered ports, not just the collision
Diagnose the installation error introduced with the Astro 6 upgrade
Teammate to fix Windows/WSL environment and learn Claude conversation resumption

essence: David and Jan clean up the vOz repo — gitignoring personal Claude settings.json and cleansing it from cache, converting handovers into a dated docs/handover audit trail with changelog, and scaffolding a new 'Deep in the Woods' project from documentation/commands (not copied live projects) with empty-by-default style guides.

actions.text: Gitignore personal settings files and cleanse them from git cache so collaborators' settings don't cross-pollute
Maintain docs/handover folder with dated handovers plus changelog as audit trail
Create Deep in the Woods project folder from documented project-setup patterns and transcribe the supplied MP3

essence: Fresh from a meeting with Ian and Martin, David excitedly explains a joint go-to-market product — a cheap push-to-talk hardware device letting Thai non-programmers voice-build custom apps (vs 20-50k baht per developer-built app), sold via Shopee/Lazada, with Ian's software already working and being shared for team collaboration.

actions.text: Buy the push-to-talk hardware device via Shopee
